title: ericameria × bolanderi
intro: Ericameria × bolanderi, also known as Bolander's goldenbrush or Bolander's ericameria, is a hybrid plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae), resulting from a cross between Ericameria arborescens and Ericameria pinifolia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and protect against oxidative stress. The health benefits of Ericameria × bolanderi include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and its ability to support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve infusions of the leaves and flowers, which can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves to harness its medicinal properties.
